title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: The value of (0.29‚Äæ√∑0.32‚Äæ)√ó6.60.95‚Äæ√∑1.05‚Äæ√ó1.2‚Äæ\frac{(0.\overline{29} \div 0.\overline{32}) \times 6.6}{0.\overline{95} \div 1.\overline{05} \times 1.\overline{2}}0.95√∑1.05√ó1.2(0.29√∑0.32)√ó6.6‚Äã‚Äã is

In a town, the present number of employed people is 40,000. If the number of employed men decreases by 4% and the number of employed women increases by 12%, the total employed population becomes 40,480. What is the present number of employed men?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Total employed=40000 Decrease in men=4% Increase in women=12% New total=40480 Formula Used: New value=Original value(1¬±Percentage100) Solution: Let number of men=x Then women=40000‚àíx 0.96x+1.12(40000‚àíx)=40480 0.96x+44800‚àí1.12x=40480 ‚àí0.16x=‚àí4320 x=43200.16=27000 Final Answer: 27000\textbf{Given:}\\\ \, \\\text{Total employed}=40000\\\ \, \\\text{Decrease in men}=4\%\\\ \, \\\text{Increase in women}=12\%\\\ \, \\\text{New total}=40480\\\ \, \\\textbf{Formula Used:}\\\ \, \\\text{New value}=\text{Original value}\left(1\pm\frac{\text{Percentage}}{100}\right)\\\ \, \\\textbf{Solution:}\\\ \, \\\text{Let number of men}=x\\\ \, \\\text{Then women}=40000-x\\\ \, \\0.96x+1.12(40000-x)=40480\\\ \, \\0.96x+44800-1.12x=40480\\\ \, \\-0.16x=-4320\\\ \, \\x=\frac{4320}{0.16}=27000\\\ \, \\\textbf{Final Answer:}\\\ \, \\27000Given: Total employed=40000 Decrease in men=4% Increase in women=12% New total=40480 Formula Used: New value=Original value(1¬±100Percentage‚Äã) Solution: Let number of men=x Then women=40000‚àíx 0.96x+1.12(40000‚àíx)=40480 0.96x+44800‚àí1.12x=40480 ‚àí0.16x=‚àí4320 x=0.164320‚Äã=27000 Final Answer: 27000 Exam Hall Method:

questions.question: A number increased by 16.66 percent, 14.28 percent, 9.09 percent and 11.11 percent respectively and after it decreased by 12.5 percent, 8.33 percent, 14.28 percent and 10 percent respectively. Find the total (approximate) change in percentage.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Successive increases: 16.66%, 14.28%, 9.09%, 11.11% Successive decreases: 12.5%, 8.33%, 14.28%, 10% Concept Used: Successive percentage change using net multiplier method Formula Used: Net change factor=(1+increase)√ó(1‚àídecrease) Solution: 16.66%=16, 14.28%=17, 9.09%=111, 11.11%=19 12.5%=18, 8.33%=112, 14.28%=17, 10%=110 Net factor=76‚ãÖ87‚ãÖ1211‚ãÖ109‚ãÖ78‚ãÖ1112‚ãÖ67‚ãÖ910 =1 Net percentage change=(1‚àí1)√ó100 =0% Final Answer: 0%\textbf{Given:} \\ \, \\\text{Successive increases: } 16.66\%,\;14.28\%,\;9.09\%,\;11.11\% \\ \, \\\text{Successive decreases: } 12.5\%,\;8.33\%,\;14.28\%,\;10\% \\ \, \\\textbf{Concept Used:} \\ \, \\\text{Successive percentage change using net multiplier method} \\ \, \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \, \\\text{Net change factor} =(1+\text{increase}) \times (1-\text{decrease}) \\ \, \\\textbf{Solution:} \\ \, \\16.66\% = \frac{1}{6},\;14.28\% = \frac{1}{7},\;9.09\% = \frac{1}{11},\;11.11\% = \frac{1}{9} \\ \, \\12.5\% = \frac{1}{8},\;8.33\% = \frac{1}{12},\;14.28\% = \frac{1}{7},\;10\% = \frac{1}{10} \\ \, \\\text{Net factor} = \frac{7}{6}\cdot\frac{8}{7}\cdot\frac{12}{11}\cdot\frac{10}{9}\cdot\frac{7}{8}\cdot\frac{11}{12}\cdot\frac{6}{7}\cdot\frac{9}{10} \\ \, \\= 1 \\ \, \\\text{Net percentage change} = (1-1)\times100 \\ \, \\= 0\% \\ \, \\\textbf{Final Answer:} \\ \, \\0\%Given:Successive increases: 16.66%,14.28%,9.09%,11.11%Successive decreases: 12.5%,8.33%,14.28%,10%Concept Used:Successive percentage change using net multiplier methodFormula Used:Net change factor=(1+increase)√ó(1‚àídecrease)Solution:16.66%=61‚Äã,14.28%=71‚Äã,9.09%=111‚Äã,11.11%=91‚Äã12.5%=81‚Äã,8.33%=121‚Äã,14.28%=71‚Äã,10%=101‚ÄãNet factor=67‚Äã‚ãÖ78‚Äã‚ãÖ1112‚Äã‚ãÖ910‚Äã‚ãÖ87‚Äã‚ãÖ1211‚Äã‚ãÖ76‚Äã‚ãÖ109‚Äã=1Net percentage change=(1‚àí1)√ó100=0%Final Answer:0%‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The solution of the following system of equations: 2(ax - by) + a + 4b = 0, 2(bx + ay) + b - 4a = 0, is also the solution of the equation:
questions.explanation: Given: System of equations: 2(ax - by) + a + 4b = 0 \implies 2ax - 2by = -a - 4b (E1) 2(bx + ay) + b - 4a = 0 \implies 2bx + 2ay = 4a - b (E2) Solution: Multiply (E1) by ( a ) and (E2) by ( b ): ‚Äãa√ó(E1):2a2x‚àí2aby=‚àía2‚àí4aba \times \text{(E1)}: 2a^2x - 2aby = -a^2 - 4ab a√ó(E1):2a2x‚àí2aby=‚àía2‚àí4ab‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äãb√ó(E2):2b2x+2aby=4ab‚àíb2 b \times \text{(E2)}: 2b^2x + 2aby = 4ab - b^2 b√ó(E2):2b2x+2aby=4ab‚àíb2‚Äã‚Äã Add the resulting equations: ‚Äã2a2x+2b2x=‚àía2‚àí4ab+4ab‚àíb22a^2x + 2b^2x = -a^2 - 4ab + 4ab - b^22a2x+2b2x=‚àía2‚àí4ab+4ab‚àíb2‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã2(a2+b2)x=‚àí(a2+b2)2(a^2 + b^2)x = -(a^2 + b^2)2(a2+b2)x=‚àí(a2+b2)‚Äã‚Äã x = ‚àí12 -\frac{1}{2}‚àí21‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Substitute x = ‚àí12 -\frac{1}{2}‚àí21‚Äã‚Äãinto (E2): ‚Äã2b(‚àí12)+2ay=4a‚àíb2b\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right) + 2ay = 4a - b2b(‚àí21‚Äã)+2ay=4a‚àíb‚Äã‚Äã -b + 2ay = 4a - b 2ay = 4a y = 2 Thus, the unique solution to the system is x, y = (‚àí12,2)\left(-\frac{1}{2}, 2\right) (‚àí21‚Äã,2) Now, checking in options; D. 4x + 3y = 4 ‚Äã4(‚àí12)+3(2)=‚àí2+6=4(equal to 4)4\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right) + 3(2) = -2 + 6 = 4 \quad (\text{equal to 4})4(‚àí21‚Äã)+3(2)=‚àí2+6=4(equal to 4)‚Äã‚Äã Thus, the correct option is D.

questions.question: If cot œÜ + tan œÜ = 4 and œÜ is an acute angle, find the value of cot¬≥ œÜ + tan¬≥ œÜ.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: cot‚Å°œï+tan‚Å°œï=4 Formula Used: a3+b3=(a+b)(a2+b2‚àíab) Alternatively, (a+b)3=a3+b3+3ab(a+b) Solution: Let a=cot‚Å°œï and b=tan‚Å°œï. We know ab=cot‚Å°œï‚ãÖtan‚Å°œï=1. We are given a+b=4. Using the identity: (a+b)3=a3+b3+3ab(a+b) (4)3=(cot‚Å°3œï+tan‚Å°3œï)+3(1)(4) 64=(cot‚Å°3œï+tan‚Å°3œï)+12 cot‚Å°3œï+tan‚Å°3œï=64‚àí12 cot‚Å°3œï+tan‚Å°3œï=52 Final Answer 52\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \cot \phi + \tan \phi = 4 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \space \, \\ a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)(a^2 + b^2 - ab) \\ \space \, \\ \text{Alternatively, } (a+b)^3 = a^3 + b^3 + 3ab(a+b) \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Let } a = \cot \phi \text{ and } b = \tan \phi. \\ \space \, \\ \text{We know } ab = \cot \phi \cdot \tan \phi = 1. \\ \space \, \\ \text{We are given } a + b = 4. \\ \space \, \\ \text{Using the identity:} \\ \space \, \\ (a + b)^3 = a^3 + b^3 + 3ab(a + b) \\ \space \, \\ (4)^3 = (\cot^3 \phi + \tan^3 \phi) + 3(1)(4) \\ \space \, \\ 64 = (\cot^3 \phi + \tan^3 \phi) + 12 \\ \space \, \\ \cot^3 \phi + \tan^3 \phi = 64 - 12 \\ \space \, \\ \cot^3 \phi + \tan^3 \phi = 52 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 52Given: cotœï+tanœï=4 Formula Used: a3+b3=(a+b)(a2+b2‚àíab) Alternatively, (a+b)3=a3+b3+3ab(a+b) Solution: Let a=cotœï and b=tanœï. We know ab=cotœï‚ãÖtanœï=1. We are given a+b=4. Using the identity: (a+b)3=a3+b3+3ab(a+b) (4)3=(cot3œï+tan3œï)+3(1)(4) 64=(cot3œï+tan3œï)+12 cot3œï+tan3œï=64‚àí12 cot3œï+tan3œï=52 Final Answer 52‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Rawesh started a business by investing ‚Çπ1,00,000. After 5 months, Suresh woined with ‚Çπ1,50,000. At the end of 7 months from the start, Tushar woined with ‚Çπ2,00,000. If the total profit is ‚Çπ1,50,000 at the end of the year, find the share of Tushar(approximate) .
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:Rawesh invests 100000 for 12 monthsSuresh invests 150000 for 7 monthsTushar invests 200000 for 5 monthsTotal profit=150000Concept Used:Partnership based on capital and timeFormula Used:Profit ratio=Capital√óTimeSolution:Rawesh=100000√ó12=1200000Suresh=150000√ó7=1050000Tushar=200000√ó5=1000000Ratio=1200000:1050000:1000000=24:21:20Sum of ratios=65Tushar‚Äôs share=2065√ó150000=46153.85Final Answer:Tushar‚Äôs share=46153.85‚âà‚Çπ46154\textbf{Given:}\\\text{Rawesh invests } 100000 \text{ for } 12 \text{ months}\\\\\text{Suresh invests } 150000 \text{ for } 7 \text{ months}\\\\\text{Tushar invests } 200000 \text{ for } 5 \text{ months}\\\\\text{Total profit} = 150000\\\\\textbf{Concept Used:}\\\text{Partnership based on capital and time}\\\\\textbf{Formula Used:}\\\text{Profit ratio} = \text{Capital} \times \text{Time}\\\\\textbf{Solution:}\\\text{Rawesh} = 100000 \times 12 = 1200000\\\\\text{Suresh} = 150000 \times 7 = 1050000\\\\\text{Tushar} = 200000 \times 5 = 1000000\\\\\text{Ratio} = 1200000 : 1050000 : 1000000\\\\= 24 : 21 : 20\\\\\text{Sum of ratios} = 65\\\\\text{Tushar's share} = \frac{20}{65} \times 150000\\\\= 46153.85\\\\\textbf{Final Answer:}\\\text{Tushar's share} = 46153.85 \approx ‚Çπ46154Given:Rawesh invests 100000 for 12 monthsSuresh invests 150000 for 7 monthsTushar invests 200000 for 5 monthsTotal profit=150000Concept Used:Partnership based on capital and timeFormula Used:Profit ratio=Capital√óTimeSolution:Rawesh=100000√ó12=1200000Suresh=150000√ó7=1050000Tushar=200000√ó5=1000000Ratio=1200000:1050000:1000000=24:21:20Sum of ratios=65Tushar‚Äôs share=6520‚Äã√ó150000=46153.85Final Answer:Tushar‚Äôs share=46153.85‚âà‚Çπ46154‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: In ‚àÜABC, two medians AD and BE intersect at G at right angles. If AD = 12 cm and BE = 9 cm, then the length of AB is equal to:
questions.explanation: Given: In triangle ABC, two medians AD and BE intersect at G at right angles. AD = 12 cm, BE = 9 cm. Formula Used: In a triangle, the centroid G divides each median in the ratio 2:1, with AG:GD = 2:1. Solution: Median AD is divided by G in the ratio 2:1, ‚ÄãAG=23√óAD,GD=13√óAD =&gtAG=23√ó12=8 cm =&gtGD=13√ó12=4 cmAG = \frac{2}{3} \times AD, \quad GD = \frac{1}{3} \times AD\\\ \\\Rightarrow AG = \frac{2}{3} \times 12 = 8 \text{ cm}\\\ \\\Rightarrow GD = \frac{1}{3} \times 12 = 4 \text{ cm}\\AG=32‚Äã√óAD,GD=31‚Äã√óAD =&gtAG=32‚Äã√ó12=8 cm =&gtGD=31‚Äã√ó12=4 cm‚Äã‚Äã Similarly, median BE is divided by G in the ratio 2:1, ‚ÄãBG=23√óBE,GE=13√óBE =&gtBG=23√ó9=6 cm =&gtGE=13√ó9=3 cmBG = \frac{2}{3} \times BE, \quad GE = \frac{1}{3} \times BE\\\ \\\Rightarrow BG = \frac{2}{3} \times 9 = 6 \text{ cm}\\\ \\\Rightarrow GE = \frac{1}{3} \times 9 = 3 \text{ cm}\\BG=32‚Äã√óBE,GE=31‚Äã√óBE =&gtBG=32‚Äã√ó9=6 cm =&gtGE=31‚Äã√ó9=3 cm‚Äã‚Äã Now, using the property that the medians of a triangle intersect at right angles, the length of side AB can be found using the Pythagorean theorem: AB¬≤ = AG¬≤ + BG¬≤ =&gt AB¬≤ = 8¬≤ + 6¬≤ =&gt AB¬≤ = 64 + 36 =&gt AB¬≤ = 100 =&gt AB = ‚àö100 = 10 cm

questions.question: The ratio of the numbers of ‚Çπ10 notes, ‚Çπ20 notes and ‚Çπ50 notes in a box is given as 3 : 5 : 7. The total amount of cash in the box is ‚Çπ3,360. Find the number of ‚Çπ20 notes in that box.
questions.explanation: Given: Ratio of ‚Çπ10 : ‚Çπ20 : ‚Çπ50 notes = 3 : 5 : 7 Total cash = ‚Çπ3360 Solution: Total Amount of 10 Rupee Notes = 10 √ó 3x = 30x Total Amount of 20 Rupee Notes = 20 √ó 5x = 100x Total Amount of 50 Rupee Notes = 50 √ó 7x = 350x Total Amount overall = 30x + 100x + 350x = 480x 480x = 3360 x = 7 Now, number of ‚Çπ20 notes = 5x = 5 √ó 7 = 35 Alternate Method: Total = 30x + 100x + 350x = 480x 480x = 3360 x = 7 Now, number of ‚Çπ20 notes = 5x = 5 √ó 7 = 35

questions.question: If Asif offers a 40 percent discount on the marked price, then he makes a loss of 16.66 percent. What is the percentage of impurities (of no cost) that has to be mixed to gain a 10 percent profit after the discount?
questions.explanation: ‚Äã‚Äã‚ÄãGiven: Discount=40% Loss=16.66% Required profit=10% Formula Used: Loss %=CP‚àíSPCP√ó100 Profit %=SP‚àíCPCP√ó100 Solution: Let CP of pure material=100 Loss 16.66%=16 SP=100√ó56=83.33 For 10% profit: CPeffective=83.331.10=75.76 Let fraction of pure material=x 100x=75.76 x=0.7576 Fraction of impurities=1‚àí0.7576=0.2424 Percentage of impurities=24.24% Final Answer: 24.24%\textbf{Given:} \\ \, \\\text{Discount} = 40\% \\ \, \\\text{Loss} = 16.66\% \\ \, \\\text{Required profit} = 10\% \\ \, \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \, \\\text{Loss \%} = \frac{CP-SP}{CP} \times 100 \\ \, \\\text{Profit \%} = \frac{SP-CP}{CP} \times 100 \\ \, \\\textbf{Solution:} \\ \, \\\text{Let CP of pure material} = 100 \\ \, \\\text{Loss } 16.66\% = \frac{1}{6} \\ \, \\SP = 100 \times \frac{5}{6} = 83.33 \\ \, \\\text{For } 10\% \text{ profit:} \\ \, \\CP_{\text{effective}} = \frac{83.33}{1.10} = 75.76 \\ \, \\\text{Let fraction of pure material} = x \\ \, \\100x = 75.76 \\ \, \\x = 0.7576 \\ \, \\\text{Fraction of impurities} = 1 - 0.7576 = 0.2424 \\ \, \\\text{Percentage of impurities} = 24.24\% \\ \, \\\textbf{Final Answer:} \\ \, \\24.24\%Given:Discount=40%Loss=16.66%Required profit=10%Formula Used:Loss %=CPCP‚àíSP‚Äã√ó100Profit %=CPSP‚àíCP‚Äã√ó100Solution:Let CP of pure material=100Loss 16.66%=61‚ÄãSP=100√ó65‚Äã=83.33For 10% profit:CPeffective‚Äã=1.1083.33‚Äã=75.76Let fraction of pure material=x100x=75.76x=0.7576Fraction of impurities=1‚àí0.7576=0.2424Percentage of impurities=24.24%Final Answer:24.24%‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The average marks of Gopal in 12 papers is 83. If he got the same highest marks in two papers and the same lowest marks in three papers, excluding the highest and lowest marks, the average of the remaining papers is 89, and the difference between the highest and the lowest marks is 39. If the highest is 2 marks less and lowest is 6 marks more, then the average of 12 papers is‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: Average of 12 papers = 83 ‚Üí Total marks = 12 √ó 83 = 996 Highest marks = H (same in 2 papers) Lowest marks = L (same in 3 papers) Avg. of remaining 7 papers = 89 ‚Üí Total of remaining = 7 √ó 89 = 623 Difference: H ‚Äì L = 39 New highest = H ‚Äì 2 New lowest = L + 6 Formula Used: Total = Average √ó Number of items Solution: Total of all papers: 996 = 2H + 3L + 623 => 2H + 3L = 373 Also given: H ‚Äì L = 39 => H = L + 39 Substitute in total equation: 2(L + 39) + 3L = 373 => 2L + 78 + 3L = 373 => 5L = 295 => L = 59 => H = 98 Change in totals: New highest = 98 ‚Äì 2 = 96 ‚Üí change = ‚Äì2 each ‚Üí total change = ‚Äì4 New lowest = 59 + 6 = 65 ‚Üí change = +6 each ‚Üí total change = +18 Net change = ‚Äì4 + 18 = +14 New total = 996 + 14 = 1010 New average = 101012=8416\frac{1010}{12} = 84\frac{1}{6}121010‚Äã=8461‚Äã‚Äã marks

questions.question: There are 80 students in a hostel. Due to new admissions, 30 new students woin the mess, and the daily expense of the mess increases by ‚Çπ260, while the average expenditure per head diminishes by ‚Çπ10. What was the original daily expenditure (in ‚Çπ, rounded off to the nearest tens) of the mess?
questions.explanation: Given: Original students = 80 New students = 30 ‚Üí Total = 110 Increase in total expense = ‚Çπ260 Average expense per head decreases by ‚Çπ10 Formula Used: Average = Total expense √∑ Number of students. Solution: Let original average expense per head = x. Then original total expense = 80x ‚Äã80x+260110=x‚àí10\frac{80x + 260}{110} = x - 1011080x+260‚Äã=x‚àí10‚Äã‚Äã 80x + 260 = 110(x - 10) 80x + 260 = 110x - 1100 260 + 1100 = 110x - 80x 1360 = 30x x =136030=45.33= \frac{1360}{30} = 45.33=301360‚Äã=45.33‚Äã‚Äã Original total daily expenditure: 80x = 80√ó45.330 \times 45.33 0√ó45.33‚Äã= 3626.4 Rounded off to nearest tens: ‚Çπ3630

questions.question: A, B and C can do a piece of work in 18 days, 24 days and 36 days, respectively. On the first day A is assisted by B, and on the second day C assists A. This 2-day cycle is repeated starting with A and B, till the work gets completed. How long in days would it take to complete the work?
questions.explanation: Given: A = 18 days B = 24 days C = 36 days Day-1: A + B work Day-2: A + C work Cycle = 2 days (repeat) Formula Used: Efficiency = WorkTime\frac{\text{Work}}{\text{Time}}TimeWork‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Total work = {LCM}(18,24,36) = 72 units Efficiencies: ‚ÄãA=7218=4,B=7224=3,C=7236=2A=\frac{72}{18}=4,\quad B=\frac{72}{24}=3,\quad C=\frac{72}{36}=2A=1872‚Äã=4,B=2472‚Äã=3,C=3672‚Äã=2‚Äã‚Äã Work on Day-1 (A + B) = 4 + 3 =7 units Work on Day-2 (A + C) = 4 + 2 = 6 units Work in 2-day cycle = 7 + 6 = 13 units Now calculate full cycles: 72 √∑ 13 = 5 cycles (65 units) Remaining work = 7 units Day-1 of next cycle (A + B) = 7 units exactly Thus, extra required = 1 day Total time = 10 + 1 = 11 days

questions.question: The price of 4 pens and 5 notebooks is ‚Çπ130. If the price of a pen increases by 25% and the price of a notebook decreases by 20%, then the price of 2 pens and 4 notebooks becomes ‚Çπ82. What is the original price of 1 pen and 2 notebooks?
questions.explanation: Solution: Let original prices be: pen = p, notebook = n. 4p + 5n = 130 Price of a pen increases by 25% and the price of a notebook decreases by 20%‚Äã After change: pen = 1.25p, notebook = 0.8n. 2(1.25p) + 4(0.8n) = 82 2.5p + 3.2n = 82 Substitution method: from 4p + 5n = 130 p = 32.5 - 1.25n. Substitute into 2.5p + 3.2n = 82. 2.5 (32.5 - 1.25n) + 3.2n = 82 81.25 - 3.125n + 3.2n = 82 81.25 + 0.075n = 82 0.075n = 0.75 n = 10. p = 32.5 - 1.25(10) = 20. Original price of (1) pen and (2) notebooks = p + 2n = 20 + 20 = ‚Çπ40. Exam Hall Method: ‚Äã

questions.question: If the 9-digit number 5947x19y2 is divisible by 88,then what is the maximum possible value of (x+y)?
questions.explanation: Given: The 9-digit number is 5947x19y2, which is divisible by 88 Concept Used: For a number to be divisible by 88, it must be divisible by both 8 and 11 Solution: Applying divisibility by 8 Try values of y from 0 to 9: y = 1: 912 √∑ 8 = 114 ‚Üí divisible y = 5: 952 √∑ 8 = 119 ‚Üí divisible y = 9: 992 √∑ 8 = 124 ‚Üí divisible Valid y values: 1, 5, 9 Applying divisibility by 11 Digits: 5 9 4 7 x 1 9 y 2 Odd positions: 5 + 4 + x + 9 + 2 = 20 + x Even positions: 9 + 7 + 1 + y = 17 + y Difference: (20 + x) - (17 + y) = x - y + 3 Must be divisible by 11 x - y + 3 = 0 x = y - 3 Now checking valid (x, y) pairs: y = 1 Then x = -2 y = 5 Then x = 2 y = 9 Then x = 6 Valid pairs: (2, 5) and (6, 9) Maximize value of x + y Taking (2, 5) = 2 + 5 = 7 Taking(6, 9) = 6 + 9 = 15 (maximum)

questions.question: The median of 9, 11, 15, 17. x. 19, 21 is x. where x is an integer. Which of the following can be the value of x?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Median of 7 number is x Formula used: Median of odd number = (n+1)2\frac{(n + 1)} 2 2(n+1)‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Numbers are; => 9, 11 , 15, 17, x, 19, 21 Total number are = 7 => Median = (7+1)2\frac{(7 + 1)} 22(7+1)‚Äã‚Äã = 82\frac8 228‚Äã‚Äã = 4th term So median will be 4th term; On rearranging the terms, we get; => Series = 9, 11, 15, x, 17, 19, 21 As we arrange it in ascending order => 15 < x < 17 So, only possible value of x is 16 => Hence, the answer is (c).

questions.question: The height of a conical toy is 12 cm and the radius of its base is 5 cm. Find the cost of painting of the toy from outside at the rate of Rs. 28 sq. cm and the painter does not paint the base of the toy. (Use œÄ = 22/7)
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Height=12 Radius=5 Rate=28 Concept Used: Curved surface area of cone=œÄrl Solution: l=122+52=13 CSA=227√ó5√ó13‚âà204.28 Cost=204.28√ó28‚âà5720 Final Answer 5720\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Height} = 12 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Radius} = 5 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Rate} = 28 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Concept Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Curved surface area of cone}= \pi rl \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ l = \sqrt{12^2 + 5^2} = 13 \\ \space \, \\ \text{CSA} = \frac{22}{7} \times 5 \times 13 \approx 204.28 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Cost} = 204.28 \times 28 \approx 5720 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 5720Given: Height=12 Radius=5 Rate=28 Concept Used: Curved surface area of cone=œÄrl Solution: l=122+52‚Äã=13 CSA=722‚Äã√ó5√ó13‚âà204.28 Cost=204.28√ó28‚âà5720 Final Answer 5720‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Distance covered by a train in the first hour is 40 km, distance covered in the second hour is 43 km, distance covered in the third hour is 46 km and so on for 10 hours. What will be the total distance covered by the train in these 10 hours?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Distances covered are 40, 43, 46, ‚Ä¶ for 10 hours Concept Used: Arithmetic Progression Formula Used: Sn=n2[2a+(n‚àí1)d] Solution: a=40 d=3 n=10 S10=102[2(40)+9√ó3] =5[80+27] =5√ó107 =535 Final Answer: 535 km\textbf{Given:} \\ \, \\\text{Distances covered are } 40,\;43,\;46,\;\dots \text{ for } 10 \text{ hours} \\ \, \\\textbf{Concept Used:} \\ \, \\\text{Arithmetic Progression} \\ \, \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \, \\S_n = \frac{n}{2}[2a + (n-1)d] \\ \, \\\textbf{Solution:} \\ \, \\a = 40 \\ \, \\d = 3 \\ \, \\n = 10 \\ \, \\S_{10} = \frac{10}{2}[2(40) + 9 \times 3] \\ \, \\= 5[80 + 27] \\ \, \\= 5 \times 107 \\ \, \\= 535 \\ \, \\\textbf{Final Answer:} \\ \, \\535 \text{ km}Given:Distances covered are 40,43,46,‚Ä¶ for 10 hoursConcept Used:Arithmetic ProgressionFormula Used:Sn‚Äã=2n‚Äã[2a+(n‚àí1)d]Solution:a=40d=3n=10S10‚Äã=210‚Äã[2(40)+9√ó3]=5[80+27]=5√ó107=535Final Answer:535 km‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Abhishek rides a bicycle around a circular path of radius 100 m. The radius of the wheel of the bicycle is 150 cm. The bicycle comes to the starting point for the first time in 1 hour 40 minutes. What is the number of revolutions of the wheel in 45 minutes?
questions.explanation: Given: Radius of path = 100 m, Radius of wheel = 150 cm. Solution: Circumference of path = 2 √ó 22 √∑ 7 √ó 100 Time for one round = 100 minutes In 45 minutes distance covered = 45 √∑ 100 √ó circumference Circumference of wheel = 2 √ó 22 √∑ 7 √ó 1.5 Number of revolutions = 30 Final Answer: So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Rohan owns a field worth of Rs. 40000. He sells it to Kawal at a profit of 10 percent. After some time, Kawal sells it back to Rohan at 5 percent loss. What is the profit or loss percent of Rohan?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:Value of field=40000Profit when sold=10%Loss when bought back=5%Formula Used:Selling Price=Cost Price√ó(1¬±Percentage100)Profit %=ProfitInitial Investment√ó100Solution:Selling Price1=40000√ó1.10=44000Buying Price2=44000√ó0.95=41800Profit=44000‚àí41800=2200Profit %=220040000√ó100=5.5%Final Answer:Rohan gains a profit of 5.5%.\textbf{Given:} \\[6pt]\text{Value of field} = 40000 \\[4pt]\text{Profit when sold} = 10\% \\[4pt]\text{Loss when bought back} = 5\% \\[12pt]\textbf{Formula Used:} \\[6pt]\text{Selling Price} = \text{Cost Price} \times \left(1 \pm \frac{\text{Percentage}}{100}\right) \\[10pt]\text{Profit \%}= \frac{\text{Profit}}{\text{Initial Investment}} \times 100 \\[12pt]\textbf{Solution:} \\[6pt]\text{Selling Price}_1= 40000 \times 1.10= 44000 \\[8pt]\text{Buying Price}_2= 44000 \times 0.95= 41800 \\[8pt]\text{Profit}= 44000 - 41800= 2200 \\[10pt]\text{Profit \%}= \frac{2200}{40000} \times 100= 5.5\% \\[12pt]\textbf{Final Answer:} \\[6pt]\text{Rohan gains a profit of } 5.5\%.Given:Value of field=40000Profit when sold=10%Loss when bought back=5%Formula Used:Selling Price=Cost Price√ó(1¬±100Percentage‚Äã)Profit %=Initial InvestmentProfit‚Äã√ó100Solution:Selling Price1‚Äã=40000√ó1.10=44000Buying Price2‚Äã=44000√ó0.95=41800Profit=44000‚àí41800=2200Profit %=400002200‚Äã√ó100=5.5%Final Answer:Rohan gains a profit of 5.5%.‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Area of a triangle with verticals at (2, 3), (-1, 0) and (2, -4) is:
questions.explanation: Given: The coordinates of the vertices of the triangle are: A(2, 3), B(-1, 0), C(2, -4) Formula Used: Area of triangle = 12‚à£x1(y2‚àíy3)+x2(y3‚àíy1)+x3(y1‚àíy2)‚à£ \frac{1}{2} \left| x_1(y_2 - y_3) + x_2(y_3 - y_1) + x_3(y_1 - y_2) \right|21‚Äã‚à£x1‚Äã(y2‚Äã‚àíy3‚Äã)+x2‚Äã(y3‚Äã‚àíy1‚Äã)+x3‚Äã(y1‚Äã‚àíy2‚Äã)‚à£‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Let A(2, 3), B(-1, 0), and C(2, -4), so: ‚Äãx1=2, y1=3, x2=‚àí1, y2=0, x3=2, y3=‚àí4x_1 = 2, \ y_1 = 3, \ x_2 = -1, \ y_2 = 0,\ x_3 = 2,\ y_3 = -4x1‚Äã=2, y1‚Äã=3, x2‚Äã=‚àí1, y2‚Äã=0, x3‚Äã=2, y3‚Äã=‚àí4‚Äã‚Äã Substituting the values into the area formula: Area = 12‚à£2(0‚àí(‚àí4))+(‚àí1)(‚àí4‚àí3)+2(3‚àí0)‚à£\frac{1}{2} \left| 2(0 - (-4)) + (-1)(-4 - 3) + 2(3 - 0) \right|21‚Äã‚à£2(0‚àí(‚àí4))+(‚àí1)(‚àí4‚àí3)+2(3‚àí0)‚à£‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã=12‚à£2(4)+(‚àí1)(‚àí7)+2(3)‚à£ =12‚à£8+7+6‚à£ =12√ó21 =212= \frac{1}{2} \left| 2(4) + (-1)(-7) + 2(3) \right|\\ \ \\= \frac{1}{2} \left| 8 + 7 + 6 \right|\\ \ \\= \frac{1}{2} \times 21 \\ \ \\ = \frac{21}{2}=21‚Äã‚à£2(4)+(‚àí1)(‚àí7)+2(3)‚à£ =21‚Äã‚à£8+7+6‚à£ =21‚Äã√ó21 =221‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã The area of the triangle is 212 \frac{21}{2}221‚Äã‚Äã square units.

questions.question: If 2 times a mother‚Äôs present age is 27 years more than 9 times her daughter‚Äôs present age, and 6 times the daughter‚Äôs present age is 9 years less than the mother‚Äôs present age, then what is the difference (in years) between the ages of the mother and the daughter?
questions.explanation: Given: Let mother‚Äôs present age = M years Let daughter‚Äôs present age = D years 2M is 27 more than 9D: 2M = 9D + 27 6D is 9 less than M: 6D = M - 9 Required: M - D (difference between their ages) Solution: From 6D = M - 9: M = 6D + 9 Substitute into (2M = 9D + 27): 2(6D + 9) = 9D + 27 12D + 18 = 9D + 27 12D - 9D = 27 - 18 3D = 9 D = 3 Now M = 6D + 9 = 6 √ó 3 + 9 = 18 + 9 = 27 Difference between their ages: M - D = 27 - 3 = 24 So, the required difference in their ages is 24 years.

questions.question: A certain sum, when invested for 3 years at 10% per annum compound interest, compounded annually, amounts to Rs. 53,240. Find the sum invested (in Rs.).
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Amount (A)=53,240 Rate (r)=10% Time (t)=3 years Formula Used: A=P(1+r100)t Solution: 53240=P(1+10100)3 53240=P(1.1)3 53240=P(1.331) P=532401.331 P=532400001331 P=40,000 Final Answer Rs. 40,000\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Amount (A)} = 53,240 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Rate (r)} = 10\% \\ \space \, \\ \text{Time (t)} = 3 \text{ years} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \space \, \\ A = P\left(1 + \frac{r}{100}\right)^t \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ 53240 = P\left(1 + \frac{10}{100}\right)^3 \\ \space \, \\ 53240 = P(1.1)^3 \\ \space \, \\ 53240 = P(1.331) \\ \space \, \\ P = \frac{53240}{1.331} \\ \space \, \\ P = \frac{53240000}{1331} \\ \space \, \\ P = 40,000 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Rs. } 40,000Given: Amount (A)=53,240 Rate (r)=10% Time (t)=3 years Formula Used: A=P(1+100r‚Äã)t Solution: 53240=P(1+10010‚Äã)3 53240=P(1.1)3 53240=P(1.331) P=1.33153240‚Äã P=133153240000‚Äã P=40,000 Final Answer Rs. 40,000‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Four varieties of wheat costing ‚Çπ30/kg, ‚Çπ45/kg, ‚Çπ60/kg and an unknown price are mixed in ratio 1:2:3:4. The mixture is sold at ‚Çπ46/kg with 15% profit. What is the cost of the fourth variety?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Costs=30,45,60,x Ratio=1:2:3:4 Selling price=46 Profit=15% Concept Used: Weighted average and profit‚Äìloss Formula Used: CP=SP1+Profit100 Average cost=‚àë(Cost√óRatio)‚àëRatios Solution: CP of mixture=461.15=40 Total ratio=1+2+3+4=10 30(1)+45(2)+60(3)+4x10=40 300+4x10=40 300+4x=400 4x=100 x=25 Final Answer: 25\textbf{Given:}\\\ \, \\\text{Costs}=30,45,60,x\\\ \, \\\text{Ratio}=1:2:3:4\\\ \, \\\text{Selling price}=46\\\ \, \\\text{Profit}=15\%\\\ \, \\\textbf{Concept Used:}\\\ \, \\\text{Weighted average and profit‚Äìloss}\\\ \, \\\textbf{Formula Used:}\\\ \, \\\text{CP}=\frac{\text{SP}}{1+\frac{\text{Profit}}{100}}\\\ \, \\\text{Average cost}=\frac{\sum(\text{Cost}\times\text{Ratio})}{\sum\text{Ratios}}\\\ \, \\\textbf{Solution:}\\\ \, \\\text{CP of mixture}=\frac{46}{1.15}=40\\\ \, \\\text{Total ratio}=1+2+3+4=10\\\ \, \\\frac{30(1)+45(2)+60(3)+4x}{10}=40\\\ \, \\\frac{300+4x}{10}=40\\\ \, \\300+4x=400\\\ \, \\4x=100\\\ \, \\x=25\\\ \, \\\textbf{Final Answer:}\\\ \, \\25Given: Costs=30,45,60,x Ratio=1:2:3:4 Selling price=46 Profit=15% Concept Used: Weighted average and profit‚Äìloss Formula Used: CP=1+100Profit‚ÄãSP‚Äã Average cost=‚àëRatios‚àë(Cost√óRatio)‚Äã Solution: CP of mixture=1.1546‚Äã=40 Total ratio=1+2+3+4=10 1030(1)+45(2)+60(3)+4x‚Äã=40 10300+4x‚Äã=40 300+4x=400 4x=100 x=25 Final Answer: 25‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If cosec¬≤Œ± = 9, then find the value of cos¬≤Œ± + sec (90-Œ±) where 0¬∞ < Œ± < 90¬∞.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: cosec‚Å°2Œ±=9 0‚àò<Œ±<90‚àò Concept Used: sin‚Å°Œ±=1cosec‚Å°Œ± cos‚Å°2Œ±=1‚àísin‚Å°2Œ± sec‚Å°(90‚àò‚àíŒ±)=cosec‚Å°Œ± Solution: From cosec‚Å°2Œ±=9, we get cosec‚Å°Œ±=3 (since Œ± is acute). sin‚Å°Œ±=13 Calculate cos‚Å°2Œ±: cos‚Å°2Œ±=1‚àí(13)2=1‚àí19=89 Calculate sec‚Å°(90‚àò‚àíŒ±): sec‚Å°(90‚àò‚àíŒ±)=cosec‚Å°Œ±=3 Now find the sum: cos‚Å°2Œ±+sec‚Å°(90‚àò‚àíŒ±)=89+3 =8+279=359 Final Answer 359\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \cosec^2 \alpha = 9 \\ \space \, \\ 0^\circ < \alpha < 90^\circ \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Concept Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \sin \alpha = \frac{1}{\cosec \alpha} \\ \space \, \\ \cos^2 \alpha = 1 - \sin^2 \alpha \\ \space \, \\ \sec(90^\circ - \alpha) = \cosec \alpha \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{From } \cosec^2 \alpha = 9, \text{ we get } \cosec \alpha = 3 \text{ (since } \alpha \text{ is acute)}. \\ \space \, \\ \sin \alpha = \frac{1}{3} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Calculate } \cos^2 \alpha: \\ \space \, \\ \cos^2 \alpha = 1 - \left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^2 = 1 - \frac{1}{9} = \frac{8}{9} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Calculate } \sec(90^\circ - \alpha): \\ \space \, \\ \sec(90^\circ - \alpha) = \cosec \alpha = 3 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Now find the sum:} \\ \space \, \\ \cos^2 \alpha + \sec(90^\circ - \alpha) = \frac{8}{9} + 3 \\ \space \, \\ = \frac{8 + 27}{9} = \frac{35}{9} \\ \space \,\\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ \frac{35}{9}Given: cosec2Œ±=9 0‚àò<Œ±<90‚àò Concept Used: sinŒ±=cosecŒ±1‚Äã cos2Œ±=1‚àísin2Œ± sec(90‚àò‚àíŒ±)=cosecŒ± Solution: From cosec2Œ±=9, we get cosecŒ±=3 (since Œ± is acute). sinŒ±=31‚Äã Calculate cos2Œ±: cos2Œ±=1‚àí(31‚Äã)2=1‚àí91‚Äã=98‚Äã Calculate sec(90‚àò‚àíŒ±): sec(90‚àò‚àíŒ±)=cosecŒ±=3 Now find the sum: cos2Œ±+sec(90‚àò‚àíŒ±)=98‚Äã+3 =98+27‚Äã=935‚Äã Final Answer 935‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If p is the fourth proportion to 4, 9 and 8, and q is the third proportional of p and 30, then find the mean proportional between 32 and q.
questions.explanation: Given: p is the fourth proportional to 4, 9, and 8. q is the third proportional of p and 30. Find the mean proportional between 32 and q Concept: ‚Äãab=cp\frac{a}{b} = \frac{c}{p}ba‚Äã=pc‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: ‚Äã49=8p\frac{4}{9} = \frac{8}{p}94‚Äã=p8‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã 4p = 72 p = 18 Find q (Third Proportional of p = 18 and 30) If q is the third proportional to a and b , then: ‚Äãab=bq\frac{a}{b} = \frac{b}{q}ba‚Äã=qb‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã1830=30q\frac{18}{30} = \frac{30}{q}3018‚Äã=q30‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã18q=90018q = 900 18q=900‚Äã‚Äã q = 50 Find Mean Proportional Between 32 and ( q = 50 ) The mean proportional between ( a ) and ( b ) is: ‚Äãa√ób\sqrt{a \times b}a√ób‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ‚ÄãMean Proportional=32√ó50=1600=40\text{Mean Proportional} = \sqrt{32 \times 50} = \sqrt{1600} = 40Mean Proportional=32√ó50‚Äã=1600‚Äã=40‚Äã‚Äã
